label: stream | flow | forest
sentence: Near a forest in a distant land, a clear stream flowed beside a mill, and nearby stood the miller’s house.

label: miller | have | daughter
sentence: The miller had a very beautiful daughter, who was also clever and sharp.

label: miller | tell | king
sentence: Proud of her, the miller told the king, who often hunted in the woods, that his daughter could spin straw into gold.

label: king | become | ∅
sentence: The king, who loved money, became greedy and ordered the girl to be brought before him.

label: he | take | she
sentence: He took her to a room filled with straw, gave her a spinning wheel, and commanded, “Spin all this straw into gold by morning, or your life is forfeit.”

label: girl | protest | ∅
sentence:
The poor girl protested that this was only her father’s boast and she could not do such a thing, but the door was locked, and she was left alone.

Sitting down in a corner, she cried for her hard fate.

label: door | open | ∅
sentence: Suddenly, the door opened, and a strange little man hobbled in.

sentence: Why are you crying?”

sentence: She told him of her impossible task.

label: he | ask | ∅
sentence: “What will you give me to spin it into gold for you?” he asked.

sentence: She offered her necklace, and he agreed.

label: he | whistle | ∅
sentence:
Sitting at the wheel, he whistled and sang:

“Round about, round about, 
Look and see!

label: ∅ | reel | gold
sentence:
Reel and spin, 
Straw to gold!”

label: wheel | turn | ∅
sentence: The wheel turned quickly, and by morning all the straw was spun into gold.

label: he | be | ∅
sentence: When the king saw the gold, he was amazed and pleased, but his greed grew stronger.

label: he | lock | daughter
sentence: He locked the miller’s daughter in a bigger room filled with more straw and ordered her to spin it all into gold by morning.

label: she | weep | ∅
sentence: Again, she wept not knowing what to do.

sentence: The little man appeared and asked, “What will you give me now?”

sentence: She offered her ring.

sentence: He took it and began to spin, singing his song, until all the straw was gold before dawn.

label: king | overjoy | ∅
sentence: The king was overjoyed but still desired more.

label: he | lead | daughter
sentence: He led the miller’s daughter to an even larger pile of straw and declared that if she spun it all by morning, she could become his queen.

label: dwarf | appear | ∅
sentence: Alone once more, the dwarf appeared and asked what she would give him this time.

label: she | have | nothing
sentence: She had nothing left but agreed to give him the first child she would bear as queen.

label: man | agree | ∅
sentence: The little man agreed, spun the straw to gold again, and the king, seeing the gold, married her, making her queen.

label: queen | be | ∅
sentence: When her first child was born, the queen was happy and forgot the dwarf and their promise.

label: man | appear | room
sentence: One day, the little man appeared in her room while she played with her baby and reminded her of the bargain.

label: she | heartbroken | ∅
sentence: She was heartbroken and offered him all her kingdom’s wealth to let her keep the child, but he refused.

label: i | give | day
sentence: Finally, moved by her tears, he said, “I will give you three days.

label: you | keep | baby
sentence: If by then you can guess my name, you may keep your baby.”

label: queen | stay | ∅
sentence: The queen stayed awake all night thinking of every name she knew and sent messengers far and wide to find unusual names.

label: she | guess | name
sentence: On the first day, when the dwarf returned, she guessed many names—Timothy, Ichabod, Benjamin, Jeremiah—but he denied each one.

label: she | try | name
sentence: On the second day, she tried funny names—Bandy-legs, Hunchback, Crook-shanks—but he rejected those too.

label: messenger | return | day
sentence: On the third day, a messenger returned with news.

sentence: While climbing a hill through the forest where fox and hare said goodnight, he had spotted a small hut.

sentence: When the little man came again, she gathered her court around her throne, the nurse beside her holding the baby as if ready to give him up.

sentence: The little man shouted, “A witch told you that!
